data.title: Weekly recap of 11 finance press releases highlights Evernorth's IPO, financial literacy data, and Millennial retirement views.
data.description: A summary of key financial updates from PR Newswire this week includes Evernorth's announcement to go public, unveiling plans to offer shares on the stock market. Additionally, recent data sheds light on financial literacy, revealing insights on Millennials' perspectives on retirement planning. This information was disclosed in New York on Oct. 24, 2025. Stay informed with the latest developments in the financial world.

data.title: GrafTech reports growth in sales volume: 9% year-over-year for Q3, 2025, and 8% in first nine months. US sales volume grew 53% in Q3.
data.description: In Brooklyn Heights, Ohio, GrafTech International Ltd. (NYSE: EAF) has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and nine months ending on September 30, 2025. The company reported a 9% year-over-year growth in sales volume for the third quarter of 2025 and an 8% increase for the first nine months of the year. In the United States specifically, sales volume grew by an impressive 53% year-over-year for the third quarter of 2025 and 39% for the first nine months of the year. These results demonstrate significant growth and success for the company in the specified period.

data.title: Target plans to cut 1,800 jobs to streamline operations and return to growth, citing internal inefficiencies.
data.description: Target Corporation, a publicly traded retail company (NYSE: TGT), is reportedly planning to reduce approximately 1,800 corporate positions in an effort to streamline operations and spur growth. This decision marks the first significant round of layoffs in ten years for the Minneapolis-based retailer. The incoming CEO, Michael Fiddelke, communicated in an internal memo that around 1,000 global corporate roles will be cut, along with the elimination of 800 open positions. Fiddelke emphasized that the company's organizational complexity has hindered progress, with too many layers and redundant tasks slowing down decision-making processes. The details of this development can be found in a report from the Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Parsons Corporation awarded $10 billion U.S. Navy logistics support contract for global operations and disaster relief.
data.description: Parsons Corporation disclosed today that they have secured a spot on the Worldwide Expeditionary Multiple Award Contract (WEXMAC), Territorial Integrity of the U.S. (TITUS) 2.1, sponsored by the U.S. Naval Supply Systems Command based in Chantilly, Virginia. The contract, valued at $10 billion, falls under the category of an Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) agreement. It is dedicated to furnishing essential supplies and services to support the operational needs of the U.S. military and allied forces, as well as aiding in humanitarian efforts and disaster response. The contract spans over a duration of five years with a provision for an additional five-year extension.

data.title: Trilogy Metals gives update on Ambler Access Project in Alaska, a 211-mile road project. (#TMQ #AAP)
data.description: Trilogy Metals Inc. (NYSE American: TMQ) (TSX: TMQ) based in Vancouver, BC, released an update on the Ambler Access Project (AAP), a planned 211-mile industrial road to the Upper Kobuk Mineral region. The road is intended for industrial use only.
